Plans by Department — Active vs Inactive

Each bar is an accounting department from the plan's hierarchy selection. A plan is Active when at least one user has made an update within the last 30 days. Inactive and Outdated plans are grouped together. Test plans excluded.

New Data Points

Requests for new data columns or metrics to be surfaced in the tool — CP metrics, contribution rates, trend signals, and similar.

Plan IDUserDateRequest Move To
20Phillip Prim2026-03-16 Cutover Session
If we had a change in freight factor, it would be awesome if I didn't have to hand calculate the increase or decrease
✅ Saved❌ Failed
5Emily Wiencek2026-03-16 Check In
Need more CP metrics
✅ Saved❌ Failed
20Phillip Prim2026-03-16 Cutover Session
GMV type would be good to have to know how much of my business was driven by like rollback sales versus regular or clearance or things like that. This would help you know the GMV that we're trying to comp last year by rollback or clearance
✅ Saved❌ Failed
5Briana Jones2026-02-25 Merch One
Yeah, we just we just a contribution metric. That'd be the two of them divided by two
✅ Saved❌ Failed
5Briana Jones2026-02-25 Cutover Session
if there's any way for us to get rest of market data in here, that something that's heavily considered when looking at new items(price gap, share). We still have to then go into a different tool to go compare to it, it takes it away from the whole point of this tool a little bit of being able to just use this one plan. It's a huge deal especially as we talk to the price gap.
✅ Saved❌ Failed
2Tommy Reed2026-02-20 Cutover Session
Can we add expand or reduce somewhere?
✅ Saved❌ Failed
5
Custom Columns & Notes

Add free-text notes and custom data columns to plans without a new field request.

Plan IDUserDateRequest Move To
20Phillip Prim2026-03-16 Cutover Session
It would be an amazing to have a row above here saying "it is by season xyz" when I filtered it out or export out of it
✅ Saved❌ Failed
14Paula Denton2026-03-03 Cutover Session
Are there open source comments columns where you can type in what you want when you export it? Like a attribute column, something we can type in for notes that we need.
✅ Saved❌ Failed
7Colton Simpson2026-02-26 Cutover Session
Would like a Notes column or Custom Attribution column for that continual stream of consciousness to track. With the comments being permanent, what if I change my mind later on down the line, it's just still on the cell. If I double my forecast by X amount, I should explain why I did that
✅ Saved❌ Failed
2Caroline Stevick2026-02-20 Cutover Session
Want an attribute column for custom attributes. Example: Cold Cereal category, everything rolls up to On The Go, but they don't look at it or plan for it that way. They look at it broken down a step further.
✅ Saved❌ Failed
2Tommy Reed2026-02-20 Cutover Session
Want the ability to put in what I think your future cost and retail is to show the bigger picture of how I am looking at things
✅ Saved❌ Failed
4
Upload & Bulk Editing

Upload item lists, re-upload changes in bulk, and edit multiple fields across many items at once.

Plan IDUserDateRequest Move To
20Allen Ayers2026-03-16 Cutover Session
It may be good to have the new item upload confirm what it mapped the data to, like as a step before populating into the plan to make sure that that the user has a second check to make sure it actually is the intended mapping
✅ Saved❌ Failed
14Brenton Dunn2026-03-03 Cutover Session
The bulk edit thing might be the my biggest obstacle as a merchant. What I do today is I have my template, my master file in Excel. I always have open and then I'll update it along the way, but it's already in the format of art so I can just upload it in bulk and it reflects. That might be challenging with this new tool.
✅ Saved❌ Failed
14Delanie Stanton2026-03-03 Cutover Session

✅ Saved❌ Failed
2Tommy Reed2026-02-20 Cutover Session
I need UPC for the store list upload template, not WIN

3

1
Inventory Projections

View projected inventory positions based on current assortment decisions and planned sell-through.

Plan IDUserDateRequest Move To
—Lyndsey Minasola2026-03-09 Cutover Session
Beginning inventory projections is the bane of our existence. No one wants to dio it. So we set each mod and pretend that we are inventory neutral, which is like the biggest problem in Walmart because you could have massive inventory and that line review should shift depending on your on-hands and what that forecast is for weeks. This is a lot of work for the planners and they don't want to do it.
✅ Saved❌ Failed

6
New Data Points
Requests for new data columns or metrics to be surfaced in the tool — CP metrics, contribution rates, trend signals, and similar.
4 unique submitters
Submitters
Briana JonesEmily WiencekPhillip PrimTommy Reed
All requests
“If we had a change in freight factor, it would be awesome if I didn't have to hand calculate the increase or decrease”
Phillip Prim · New Data Points · Plan 20 · 2026-03-16 Cutover Session
“Need more CP metrics”
Emily Wiencek · New Data Points · Plan 5 · 2026-03-16 Check In
“GMV type would be good to have to know how much of my business was driven by like rollback sales versus regular or clearance or things like that. This would help you know the GMV that we're trying to comp last year by rollback or clearance”
Phillip Prim · New Data Points · Plan 20 · 2026-03-16 Cutover Session
“Yeah, we just we just a contribution metric. That'd be the two of them divided by two”
Briana Jones · New Data Points · Plan 5 · 2026-02-25 Merch One
“if there's any way for us to get rest of market data in here, that something that's heavily considered when looking at new items(price gap, share). We still have to then go into a different tool to go compare to it, it takes it away from the whole point of this tool a little bit of being able to just use this one plan. It's a huge deal especially as we talk to the price gap.”
Briana Jones · New Data Points · Plan 5 · 2026-02-25 Cutover Session
“Can we add expand or reduce somewhere?”
Tommy Reed · New Data Points · Plan 2 · 2026-02-20 Cutover Session
5
Custom Columns & Notes
Add free-text notes and custom data columns to plans without a new field request.
5 unique submitters
Submitters
Caroline StevickColton SimpsonPaula DentonPhillip PrimTommy Reed
All requests
“It would be an amazing to have a row above here saying "it is by season xyz" when I filtered it out or export out of it”
Phillip Prim · Custom Columns & Notes · Plan 20 · 2026-03-16 Cutover Session
“Are there open source comments columns where you can type in what you want when you export it? Like a attribute column, something we can type in for notes that we need.”
Paula Denton · Custom Columns & Notes · Plan 14 · 2026-03-03 Cutover Session
“Would like a Notes column or Custom Attribution column for that continual stream of consciousness to track. With the comments being permanent, what if I change my mind later on down the line, it's just still on the cell. If I double my forecast by X amount, I should explain why I did that”
Colton Simpson · Custom Columns & Notes · Plan 7 · 2026-02-26 Cutover Session
“Want an attribute column for custom attributes. Example: Cold Cereal category, everything rolls up to On The Go, but they don't look at it or plan for it that way. They look at it broken down a step further.”
Caroline Stevick · Custom Columns & Notes · Plan 2 · 2026-02-20 Cutover Session
“Want the ability to put in what I think your future cost and retail is to show the bigger picture of how I am looking at things”
Tommy Reed · Custom Columns & Notes · Plan 2 · 2026-02-20 Cutover Session
4
Upload & Bulk Editing
Upload item lists, re-upload changes in bulk, and edit multiple fields across many items at once.
4 unique submitters
Submitters
Allen AyersBrenton DunnDelanie StantonTommy Reed
All requests
“It may be good to have the new item upload confirm what it mapped the data to, like as a step before populating into the plan to make sure that that the user has a second check to make sure it actually is the intended mapping”
Allen Ayers · Upload & Bulk Editing · Plan 20 · 2026-03-16 Cutover Session
“The bulk edit thing might be the my biggest obstacle as a merchant. What I do today is I have my template, my master file in Excel. I always have open and then I'll update it along the way, but it's already in the format of art so I can just upload it in bulk and it reflects. That might be challenging with this new tool.”
Brenton Dunn · Upload & Bulk Editing · Plan 14 · 2026-03-03 Cutover Session
“I think not being able to have the export and upload is really a challenge because what if we can't find auto electronics for example, with and without the space at the end until you export it, and say count the character count for all and fix it?”
Delanie Stanton · Upload & Bulk Editing · Plan 14 · 2026-03-03 Cutover Session
“I need UPC for the store list upload template, not WIN”
Tommy Reed · Upload & Bulk Editing · Plan 2 · 2026-02-20 Cutover Session

Forecast — % of Keep items where the system-generated forecast was trusted: either untouched, or adjusted by ≤3% across all forecast fields.  ·  Like-Item — % of New items where the recommended like-item reference was accepted as suggested.  ·  Buy Qty — % of Keep items where buy quantity fields were trusted: either untouched, or adjusted by ≤5%.

Signals: Walk Soon = walk or final date within 30d • Decided = >70% of items have a Keep / Add / Delete decision • WINs = at least one item has a WIN assigned • See the Buy Quantification and FAIC tabs to review override rates for these plans.

Buy Quantification

Tracks how often buyers accept AP's recommended buy quantities across all non-test plans. Accepted = exact match to the recommendation. Expand a department to see category-group detail and export overrides to CSV.

Accepted = buyer value exactly matches the AP recommendation. Overridden = buyer entered a different value. Going below the rec risks lost sales; going above risks markdown liability.
Store / FC Buy Qty
Total units to order for the season, per channel. Derived from target sell-through rate and store velocity tiers.
Store / FC Initial Set
Units allocated for the opening floor set at launch. Reflects visual merchandising intent and shelf / FC capacity -- separate from the seasonal buy.

123, 124, 135, 137, 145, 146, 151, 189 +24 more
FAIC -- Forecast Adoption

Tracks whether buyers accept AP's FAIC (Forecasting All Items in Catalog) forecast recommendations. FAIC generates a most-likely, conservative, and optimistic demand forecast for every item. AP shows the most-likely value -- what you see here is whether buyers accepted or overrode it. Click a department to see category-group breakdown.

Accepted = buyer forecast matches the AP / FAIC most-likely recommendation. Overridden = buyer entered a different value. FAIC conservative and optimistic bounds feed into PO guardrails -- large overrides may bypass those protections.
USW -- Units / Store / Week
FAIC demand forecast for the store channel (in-store sales + pickup orders).
UW FC -- Fulfillment Center
Unit Weeks for eComm orders shipped from FC (buy-it-shipped + scheduled delivery).
UW DSV -- Drop-Ship Vendor
Unit Weeks for eComm orders fulfilled by suppliers directly (DSV / drop-ship).
96.7%
USW Forecast
105,100 / 108,676 accepted
97.2%
UW FC Forecast
38,187 / 39,279 accepted
97.5%
UW DSV Forecast
2,270 / 2,329 accepted

Wins & Positive Feedback

SharePoint Live

25 pieces of feedback · 5 win types

General 11
Oral Care
"I'm so excited for this tool. I love it"
— Tosha Crawford
Dolls
"This will take away our true line plan review."
— Lyndsey Minasola
Dolls
"This is perfect. We can actually execute our plan. This is so good."
— Lyndsey Minasola
Dolls
"This does so much to take away from the day-to-day."
— Lyndsey Minasola
Dolls
"You should win an award because like, this is amazing. Team of the year"
— Lyndsey Minasola
Bakeware
"Thank you. This is so exciting. I'm so excited."
— Megan McCurdy
Bakeware
"I'm excited to use it. So much better than that of the old tool."
— Philip Prim
Pillows Poufs
"It is a great tool and will help drive better performance"
— Shay Schipper
Pillows Poufs
"I don't typically like new tools, but this one I appreciate"
— Shay Schipper
Home Reno
"Good job guys, this is way better than doing it manually"
— Brookston Jeppson
Pool Chemicals
"I'm super excited about this tool"
— Chris Nowlin
Populated data 5
Coffee
"I like that the tool gives you the comparable pieces compactly"
— Tommy Reed
Coffee
"grabbing the current price is a good thing vs the old tool that would give you an average"
— Tommy Reed
Oral Care
"LY is populated for us, and a prebuilt forecast for us. This sounds like a really awesome tool to also do your monthly forecast for OTB."
— Colton Simpson
Bakeware
"Freight Factor and other data points are pulled in automatically"
— Philip Prim
Nutrition
"Love that you can use the recommended baseline or remove and add your own"
— Britney Angela
Centralized space 4
Coffee
"I think this tool gets us better, in regards to how to get the assortment put together."
— Tommy Reed
Oral Care
"My biggest complaint with our current processes is that I have my file, my AM has her file, we combine and then I'm still in my file and they're in the shared file and then we're not synced up anymore. With this tool there will never be a separate phase or file"
— Colton Simpson
Snack Cakes
"Thank you for all the work on this. I I hope you guys know how much it's appreciated, there's a lot of work that we usually do on our end, and so it's appreciated to have tools that can help bridge some of those gaps."
— Briana Jones
Dolls
"This is one source, one-stop place."
— Keri Ann Hutchinson
Roll Ups 3
Auto Batteries
"It's good that there isn't a predefined leadership roll up because every leader wants something a little bit different"
— Paula Denton
Coffee
"love there there is flexibility in the backside that you can customize based upon leadership style"
— Tommy Reed
Snack Cakes
"The row grouping/in tool pivot table is cool"
— Emily Wiencek
Tool Function 2
Oral Care
"The bulk remove function is so legit for removing my offshore items"
— Colton Simpson
Coffee
"The date selection is easier now than what it was before."
— Tommy Reed

Buyers from the Regular Mods Only sheet whose Mod Process Start Wk falls within the 3 weeks leading up to (and including) the training date. Non-Red RYG only. Anyone with an upcoming mod is included regardless of prior training attendance — this is a monthly reminder to keep buyers engaged with the tool.
